Repo: C:/Users/Admin/Desktop/mandarin_reys_hisobot — Telegram Mini App: FastAPI backend (app/db.py, app/server.py) + vanilla-JS frontend (webapp/js/app.js, webapp/index.html, webapp/css/styles.css). Run "git diff" to see the change under review.

The diff adds:
BACKEND
- db.py: add_reys/adjust now return entry_id (lastrowid). New: _get_entry, _apply_balances (all-or-nothing per-type deltas, raises InsufficientStock if any balance would drop below -1e-9), edit_reys (reverse old net, apply new), edit_adjust (reverse old transfer, apply new), delete_entry (undo effect + delete row), ActivityNotFound.
- server.py: PUT /api/report/{entry_id}, PUT /api/adjust/{entry_id} (multipart, same validation as the POSTs), DELETE /api/entry/{entry_id}?report_id= (auth _auth_or_403 state_changing, rate-limited). 404 ActivityNotFound, 409 InsufficientStock.
FRONTEND
- Entries lists per section: {top, topchiqgan, bizda, chiqgan, reys, adjust}; reys/adjust saves push {id: json.entry_id, ..., files, ts}.
- "Yuklanganlar" buttons on both work-area tabs (reysViewBtn/adjViewBtn) → shared paginated viewer keyed by new viewerSection var (was state.formSection).
- Kebab edit: startEditReys (loads type/coef via setCoefUI/weight/photos into tab 1; editingReys; save PUTs), startEditAdjust (tab 2; editingAdj; save PUTs; adjSaveBtn shows "Yangilash: from → to"). deleteEntry is now async: for reys/adjust it DELETEs /api/entry first, updates balances, then splices locally.
- onSave/onAdjustSave: editing branches (snapshot const editing = editingReys/editingAdj before await; Object.assign entry on success; setBusy shows Yangilash while editingReys).
- Lightbox rework: lb state {mode:'form'|'entry', pk?, files?, caption?, i}; lbShow/lbNav; prev/next buttons; counter; caption (entryCaption from entry fields); swipe (touchstart/touchend dx>48); ArrowLeft/ArrowRight keyboard (skipped when focus in INPUT/TEXTAREA; also pages the entries viewer); deleteFromLightbox only in form mode (removes current, clamps index, closes when empty); entry mode uses one ephemeral object URL (lbTempUrl) revoked on switch/close.

Focus ONLY on the changed behavior. Read the files (and git diff) yourself. The Obshiy sections being session-memory-only is BY DESIGN. Backend already passes a 15-case TestClient suite (create/edit/delete/409 guards/404s/cross-report scoping) — look for what those tests DON'T cover.


You are a skeptical reviewer. Hunt for backend correctness bugs in db.edit_reys/edit_adjust/delete_entry/_apply_balances and the new endpoints: float accumulation vs the -1e-9 epsilon; same-type collisions in edit_adjust deltas (e.g. new from == old to); editing an adjust so from_type==old from; concurrent-lock assumptions; activity row fields left stale (ts/actor); DELETE /api/entry auth path (header initData vs cookie origin) vs how the frontend calls it; rate-limit key choice; report_id type coercion; whether PUT validation diverges from POST anywhere that matters.

Report concrete, real defects only (file:line, failing scenario reachable through real UI/API paths, and why). If nothing solid, return an empty list.

Self-Hosted covers certificate expiry monitoring, node/CA/client cert rotation, and Kubernetes cert management. Advanced/BYOC covers managed TLS (no action) and CMEK (Customer-Managed Encryption Key) rotation in your KMS. Standard and Basic have fully managed TLS and encryption with no customer action. CMEK is only available on Advanced. Use when monitoring cert health, performing rotation, managing CMEK, or responding to key compromise.
- cockroachdb:managing-cluster-capacity: Manages CockroachDB cluster capacity across all tiers. Self-Hosted covers node decommissioning for permanent removal and adding nodes for expansion. Advanced/BYOC covers scaling node count and machine size via Cloud Console, API, or Terraform. Standard covers adjusting provisioned compute (vCPUs). Basic auto-scales — guidance covers spending limits and cost management. Use when scaling capacity up or down, permanently removing nodes, or managing costs.
- cockroachdb:managing-cluster-settings: Reviews, audits, and modifies CockroachDB cluster settings. Self-Hosted has full control over all settings and start flags. Advanced/BYOC can modify most SQL-level settings but infrastructure settings are managed by CRL. Standard has limited settings access — session variables are the primary tuning mechanism. Basic has minimal settings — use session variables and Cloud Console. Use when auditing configuration, tuning performance, or troubleshooting settings-related issues.
- cockroachdb:performing-cluster-maintenance: Manages planned cluster maintenance across all tiers. Self-Hosted covers node drain procedures for OS patching, hardware changes, and configuration updates. Advanced/BYOC covers maintenance window configuration, patch scheduling, deferral policies, and monitoring during CRL-managed maintenance. Standard and Basic maintenance is fully managed with no customer action. Use when planning maintenance, configuring maintenance windows, or preparing applications for maintenance events.
- cockroachdb:provisioning-cluster-for-production: Guides initial CockroachDB cluster provisioning and production deployment. Self-Hosted covers cockroach start/init, Kubernetes deployment (Operator, Helm), hardware sizing, and production configuration. Advanced/BYOC covers Cloud Console, API, and Terraform provisioning with production settings. Standard covers cluster creation and provisioned compute selection. Basic covers cluster creation and spending limits. Use when creating a new cluster, preparing for production go-live, or validating deployment configuration.
- cockroachdb:reviewing-cluster-health: Performs a comprehensive health check of a CockroachDB cluster. Gathers deployment context first, then provides tier-appropriate diagnostics. Self-Hosted uses SQL against node-level system tables and CLI. Advanced/BYOC use Cloud Console and SQL with node visibility. Standard monitors provisioned compute and workload via Cloud Console. Basic monitors Request Unit consumption and connectivity. Use for daily checks, pre-maintenance validation, post-incident verification, or production readiness assessment.
- cockroachdb:upgrading-cluster-version: Guides CockroachDB version upgrades with tier-appropriate procedures. Self-Hosted covers manual rolling binary replacement with finalization control. Advanced/BYOC covers Console-initiated major upgrades, maintenance windows for patches, and release channel selection. Standard and Basic upgrades are fully automatic with no customer action required. Use when planning, executing, or monitoring a version upgrade.
- cockroachdb:cockroachdb-sql: Use when writing, generating, or optimizing SQL for CockroachDB, designing CockroachDB schemas, or when the user asks about CockroachDB-specific SQL patterns, type mappings, and distributed database best practices. Also use when encountering CockroachDB anti-patterns like missing primary keys, sequential ID hotspots, or incorrect type usage.
- cockroachdb:auditing-cloud-cluster-security: Audits the security posture of a CockroachDB cluster (Cloud or self-hosted) across network, authentication, authorization, encryption, audit logging, and backup dimensions. Use when assessing cluster security readiness, preparing for compliance reviews, or investigating security configuration gaps.
- cockroachdb:configuring-audit-logging: Configures SQL audit logging on CockroachDB clusters to capture security-relevant events including authentication, privilege changes, and sensitive data access. Use when enabling audit logging for compliance, setting up role-based audit policies, or verifying audit configuration.
- cockroachdb:configuring-ip-allowlists: Configures and hardens IP allowlists for CockroachDB Cloud clusters to restrict network access to authorized CIDR ranges. Use when tightening network security, removing overly permissive allowlist entries like 0.0.0.0/0, or setting up allowlists for a new cluster.
- cockroachdb:configuring-log-export: Configures log and metric export for CockroachDB Cloud clusters to external monitoring services including AWS CloudWatch, GCP Cloud Logging, and Datadog. Use when setting up log export for audit compliance, configuring metric export for monitoring, or troubleshooting log delivery issues.
- cockroachdb:configuring-private-connectivity: Configures private network connectivity for CockroachDB Cloud clusters including AWS PrivateLink, GCP Private Service Connect, Azure Private Link, egress private endpoints, and VPC peering. Use when setting up private endpoints to eliminate public internet exposure, configuring egress to external services like Kafka, or establishing VPC peering.
- cockroachdb:configuring-sso-and-scim: Configures SSO authentication and SCIM 2.0 provisioning for CockroachDB across four distinct layers — Cloud Console SSO (SAML/OIDC), DB Console SSO (OIDC), SQL/Cluster SSO (JWT or LDAP/AD), and SCIM 2.0 automated provisioning. Use when enabling centralized identity management, setting up SSO for compliance, or automating user lifecycle management.

Use when enabling CMEK for compliance, rotating encryption keys, or verifying CMEK configuration.

+# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
+# Entry edit / delete (fix a saved reys/adjust; inventory is compensated)
+# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
+_EPS = 1e-9  # float compensation slack: don't 409 on rounding dust
+
+
+def _get_entry(c: sqlite3.Connection, report_id: int, entry_id: int, action: str):
+    row = c.execute(
+        "SELECT * FROM activity WHERE id = ? AND report_id = ? AND action = ?",
+        (entry_id, report_id, action),
+    ).fetchone()
+    if row is None:
+        raise ActivityNotFound(entry_id)
+    return row
+
+
+def _apply_balances(c: sqlite3.Connection, report_id: int, deltas: dict[str, float]) -> dict[str, float]:
+    """Apply per-type deltas; raise InsufficientStock if any balance would go
+    negative. All-or-nothing (checked before any write)."""
+    now = int(time.time())
+    for t in deltas:
+        _ensure_type(c, report_id, t)
+    inv = _inventory(c, report_id)
+    for t, d in deltas.items():
+        if inv.get(t, 0) + d < -_EPS:
+            raise InsufficientStock(t, inv.get(t, 0), -d)
+    for t, d in deltas.items():
+        if d:
+            c.execute(
+                "UPDATE inventory SET weight = weight + ?, updated_at = ? WHERE report_id = ? AND tovar_turi = ?",
+                (d, now, report_id, t),
+            )
+    return _inventory(c, report_id)
+
+
+def edit_reys(report_id: int, entry_id: int, tovar_turi: str, weight: float,
+              coefficient: float, net: float, photos: int) -> dict:
+    if not (math.isfinite(weight) and math.isfinite(coefficient) and math.isfinite(net)):
+        raise ValueError("non-finite value")
+    with _db() as c:
+        old = _get_entry(c, report_id, entry_id, "reys")
+        deltas: dict[str, float] = {}
+        deltas[old["tovar_turi"]] = deltas.get(old["tovar_turi"], 0) - (old["net"] or 0)
+        deltas[tovar_turi] = deltas.get(tovar_turi, 0) + net
+        balances = _apply_balances(c, report_id, deltas)
+        c.execute(
+            "UPDATE activity SET tovar_turi = ?, weight = ?, coefficient = ?, net = ?, photos = ? WHERE id = ?",
+            (tovar_turi, weight, coefficient, net, photos, entry_id),
+        )
+        return {"balance": balances.get(tovar_turi, 0), "inventory": balances}
+
+
+def edit_adjust(report_id: int, entry_id: int, from_type: str, to_type: str,
+                weight: float, photos: int) -> dict:
+    if not (math.isfinite(weight) and weight > 0):
+        raise ValueError("non-finite value")
+    with _db() as c:
+        old = _get_entry(c, report_id, entry_id, "adjust")
+        deltas: dict[str, float] = {}
+        # Reverse the old transfer, then apply the new one.
+        for t, d in ((old["from_type"], old["weight"] or 0), (old["to_type"], -(old["weight"] or 0)),
+                     (from_type, -weight), (to_type, weight)):
+            deltas[t] = deltas.get(t, 0) + d
+        balances = _apply_balances(c, report_id, deltas)
+        c.execute(
+            "UPDATE activity SET from_type = ?, to_type = ?, weight = ?, photos = ? WHERE id = ?",
+            (from_type, to_type, weight, photos, entry_id),
+        )
+        return {"balances": balances}
+
+
+def delete_entry(report_id: int, entry_id: int) -> dict:
+    """Delete a reys/adjust entry and undo its inventory effect."""
+    with _db() as c:
+        row = c.execute(
+            "SELECT * FROM activity WHERE id = ? AND report_id = ?", (entry_id, report_id)
+        ).fetchone()
+        if row is None:
+            raise ActivityNotFound(entry_id)
+        if row["action"] == "reys":
+            deltas = {row["tovar_turi"]: -(row["net"] or 0)}
+        else:  # adjust: give the weight back to from_type, take it from to_type
+            deltas = {row["from_type"]: row["weight"] or 0, row["to_type"]: -(row["weight"] or 0)}
+        balances = _apply_balances(c, report_id, deltas)
+        c.execute("DELETE FROM activity WHERE id = ?", (entry_id,))
+        return {"balances": balances}

47	MAX_TOTAL_BYTES = 60 * 1024 * 1024       # 60 MB per request
48	MAX_BODY_BYTES = MAX_TOTAL_BYTES + 2 * 1024 * 1024  # + multipart overhead

56	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
57	# Reject oversized request bodies BEFORE FastAPI buffers/spools them (the
58	# per-file caps below only apply after auth, during the manual read loop).
59	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
60	@app.middleware("http")
61	async def _limit_body(request: Request, call_next):
62	    if request.method == "POST":
63	        cl = request.headers.get("content-length")
64	        if cl and cl.isdigit() and int(cl) > MAX_BODY_BYTES:
65	            return JSONResponse(status_code=413, content={"ok": False, "detail": "request too large"})
66	    return await call_next(request)

69	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
70	# Fixed-window rate limiter (bounded; keyed on a validated client IP).
71	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
72	_RATE: dict[str, tuple[int, float]] = {}
73	_RATE_MAX_KEYS = 10_000
74	
75	
76	def _rate_ok(key: str, limit: int, window: int = 60) -> bool:
77	    now = time.time()
78	    if len(_RATE) > _RATE_MAX_KEYS:  # opportunistic eviction of expired windows
79	        for k, (_, start) in list(_RATE.items()):
80	            if now - start > window:
81	                _RATE.pop(k, None)
82	    count, start = _RATE.get(key, (0, now))
83	    if now - start > window:
84	        count, start = 0, now
85	    count += 1
86	    _RATE[key] = (count, start)
87	    return count <= limit
88	
89	
90	_LOOPBACK = {"127.0.0.1", "::1"}
91	
92	
93	def _client_ip(request: Request) -> str:
94	    """Real client IP for rate limiting.
95	
96	    Honor X-Forwarded-For / CF-Connecting-IP only from a trusted source: a
97	    configured proxy, OR a loopback peer. A local tunnel (cloudflared) connects
98	    from 127.0.0.1 and only local processes can do so, so trusting loopback is
99	    safe and avoids collapsing every external client into one global bucket.
100	    """
101	    direct = request.client.host if request.client else "unknown"
102	    if direct in config.TRUSTED_PROXIES or direct in _LOOPBACK:
103	        cf = request.headers.get("cf-connecting-ip")
104	        if cf:
105	            return cf.strip()
106	        xff = request.headers.get("x-forwarded-for")
107	        if xff:
108	            return xff.split(",")[0].strip()
109	    return direct

112	def _same_origin(request: Request) -> bool:
113	    """For cookie-authenticated POSTs: require Origin/Referer to match Host (CSRF)."""
114	    host = request.headers.get("host", "")
115	    src = request.headers.get("origin") or request.headers.get("referer")
116	    if not src:
117	        return False  # a browser sends Origin on cross-origin POST; absence is suspicious
118	    return urlparse(src).netloc == host

146	async def _read_capped(upload: UploadFile, remaining_total: int) -> bytes:
147	    """Read an UploadFile in chunks, enforcing per-file and total caps."""
148	    buf = bytearray()
149	    while True:
150	        chunk = await upload.read(_CHUNK)
151	        if not chunk:
152	            break
153	        buf.extend(chunk)
154	        if len(buf) > MAX_PHOTO_BYTES:

156	        if len(buf) > remaining_total:

330	def _identity(request: Request, init_data: str = "", state_changing: bool = True) -> str:
331	    """Return an identity from initData (Telegram) or session cookie (browser).
332	
333	    Telegram initData may arrive in the form body (POST) or the
334	    `X-Telegram-Init-Data` header (GET). The same-origin (CSRF) check applies
335	    only to cookie-authenticated state-changing requests.
336	    """
337	    idata = init_data or request.headers.get("x-telegram-init-data", "")
338	    if idata:
339	        return f"tg:{authenticate_admin(idata).id}"  # raises on failure
340	    if state_changing and not _same_origin(request):
341	        raise InitDataError("bad origin")
342	    user = verify_session(request.cookies.get(SESSION_COOKIE, ""))
343	    if user is None:
344	        raise InitDataError("not authenticated")
345	    return f"pw:{user}"
346

348	def _auth_or_403(request: Request, init_data: str = "", state_changing: bool = True) -> str:
349	    try:
350	        return _identity(request, init_data, state_changing)
351	    except InitDataError as exc:
352	        raise HTTPException(status_code=403, detail=str(exc))

407	async def submit_report(
408	    request: Request,
409	    init_data: str = Form(""),
410	    report_id: int = Form(...),
411	    type: str = Form(...),
412	    coefficient: float = Form(...),
413	    coefficient_mode: str = Form(...),
414	    weight: float = Form(...),
415	    photos: list[UploadFile] = [],  # noqa: B006 (FastAPI handles default)
416	):
417	    if not _rate_ok(f"report:{_client_ip(request)}", limit=30, window=60):
418	        raise HTTPException(status_code=429, detail="too many requests")
419	
420	    identity = _auth_or_403(request, init_data)
421	    rid = _require_report(report_id)
422	
423	    tovar_turi = (type or "").strip()
424	    if not tovar_turi:
425	        raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail="tovar turi tanlanmagan")
426	    # Reject inf/nan before any arithmetic hits the DB (would poison a balance).
427	    if not (math.isfinite(weight) and math.isfinite(coefficient)):
428	        raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail="qiymat noto'g'ri")
429	    if not (weight > 0):
430	        raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail="og'irlik noto'g'ri")
431	    if coefficient < 0:
432	        raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail="koeffitsient noto'g'ri")
433	    # net weight added to inventory = weight − coefficient (coef is kg to subtract).
434	    net = round(weight - coefficient, 4)
435	    if net < 0:
436	        raise HTTPException(status_code=400, detail="koeffitsient og'irlikdan katta")
437	
438	    # Photo count cap (client cap is not trusted); read within size caps.
439	    if len(photos) > MAX_PHOTOS:
440	        raise HTTPException(status_code=413, detail=f"max {MAX_PHOTOS} photos")
441	    total = 0
442	    for f in photos:
443	        content = await _read_capped(f, MAX_TOTAL_BYTES - total)
444	        total += len(content)
445	
446	    result = db.add_reys(rid, identity, tovar_turi, weight, coefficient, net, len(photos))
447	    log.info("reys by %s [r%s]: %s +%s (weight=%s coef=%s photos=%d)",
448	             identity, rid, tovar_turi, net, weight, coefficient, len(photos))
449	    return JSONResponse({"ok": True, "net": net, "balance": result["balance"],
450	                         "inventory": result["inventory"], "entry_id": result["entry_id"]})

1	"""SQLite store. Multi-report model.
2	
3	- `reports`: named report containers (max 5; oldest auto-pruned).
4	- `inventory`: running weight balance per (report, tovar turi). Each new report
5	  starts every type at 0.
6	- `activity`: append-only log of every reys/adjust, scoped to a report.
7	
8	Single-process app, low volume: one serialized connection guarded by a lock.
9	"""
10	from __future__ import annotations
11	
12	import math
13	import sqlite3
14	import threading
15	import time
16	from contextlib import contextmanager
17	
18	from . import config
19	
20	_DB = config.DATA_DIR / "reys.db"
21	_LOCK = threading.Lock()
22	
23	DEFAULT_TYPES = ["akb", "triton", "izi", "navo", "xabib", "jet", "jon"]
24	MAX_REPORTS = 5
25	
26	
27	class InsufficientStock(Exception):
28	    def __init__(self, tovar_turi: str, have: float, need: float):
29	        self.tovar_turi = tovar_turi
30	        self.have = have
31	        self.need = need
32	        super().__init__(f"insufficient stock in {tovar_turi}: have {have}, need {need}")
33	
34	
35	class DuplicateName(Exception):
36	    pass
37	
38	
39	class ReportNotFound(Exception):
40	    pass
41	
42	
43	class ActivityNotFound(Exception):
44	    pass
45	
46	
47	def _connect() -> sqlite3.Connection:
48	    config.DATA_DIR.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
49	    conn = sqlite3.connect(_DB)
50	    conn.row_factory = sqlite3.Row
51	    conn.execute("PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL")
52	    return conn
53	
54	
55	@contextmanager
56	def _db():
57	    with _LOCK:
58	        conn = _connect()
59	        try:
60	            yield conn
61	            conn.commit()
62	        finally:
63	            conn.close()
64	
65	
66	def init() -> None:
67	    with _db() as c:
68	        # Migration: the pre-multi-report schema had global inventory/activity
69	        # (no report_id). Those rows can't map to the per-report model, so drop
70	        # the incompatible tables and recreate them fresh.
71	        for tbl in ("inventory", "activity"):
72	            cols = {r[1] for r in c.execute(f"PRAGMA table_info({tbl})")}
73	            if cols and "report_id" not in cols:
74	                c.execute(f"DROP TABLE {tbl}")
75	
76	        c.execute(
77	            """CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS reports(
78	                 id         INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
79	                 name       TEXT NOT NULL UNIQUE,
80	                 created_at INTEGER NOT NULL)"""
81	        )
82	        c.execute(
83	            """CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS inventory(
84	                 report_id  INTEGER NOT NULL,
85	                 tovar_turi TEXT NOT NULL,
86	                 weight     REAL NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
87	                 updated_at INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0,
88	                 PRIMARY KEY (report_id, tovar_turi))"""
89	        )
90	        c.execute(
91	            """CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS activity(
92	                 id          INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
93	                 report_id   INTEGER NOT NULL,
94	                 ts          INTEGER NOT NULL,
95	                 actor       TEXT NOT NULL,
96	                 action      TEXT NOT NULL,       -- 'reys' | 'adjust'
97	                 tovar_turi  TEXT,
98	                 from_type   TEXT,
99	                 to_type     TEXT,
100	                 weight      REAL,
101	                 coefficient REAL,
102	                 net         REAL,
103	                 photos      INTEGER)"""
104	        )
105	        c.execute("CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_activity_report ON activity(report_id, id)")
106	
107	        # Self-heal any non-finite (inf/nan/null) values from before the guards.
108	        fin = "({c} IS NOT NULL AND {c} > -1e308 AND {c} < 1e308)"
109	        c.execute(f"UPDATE inventory SET weight = 0 WHERE NOT {fin.format(c='weight')}")

197	    if not (math.isfinite(weight) and math.isfinite(coefficient) and math.isfinite(net)):
198	        raise ValueError("non-finite value")
199	    now = int(time.time())
200	    with _db() as c:
201	        _ensure_type(c, report_id, tovar_turi)
202	        c.execute(
203	            "UPDATE inventory SET weight = weight + ?, updated_at = ? WHERE report_id = ? AND tovar_turi = ?",
204	            (net, now, report_id, tovar_turi),
205	        )
206	        cur = c.execute(
207	            """INSERT INTO activity(report_id, ts, actor, action, tovar_turi, weight, coefficient, net, photos)
208	               VALUES(?, ?, ?, 'reys', ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)""",
209	            (report_id, now, actor, tovar_turi, weight, coefficient, net, photos),
210	        )

213	            (report_id, tovar_turi),

219	def adjust(report_id: int, actor: str, from_type: str, to_type: str, weight: float,
220	           photos: int = 0) -> dict:
221	    if not (math.isfinite(weight) and weight > 0):
222	        raise ValueError("non-finite value")
223	    now = int(time.time())
224	    with _db() as c:
225	        _ensure_type(c, report_id, from_type)
226	        _ensure_type(c, report_id, to_type)
227	        have = c.execute(
228	            "SELECT weight FROM inventory WHERE report_id = ? AND tovar_turi = ?",
229	            (report_id, from_type),
230	        ).fetchone()["weight"]
231	        if have < weight:
232	            raise InsufficientStock(from_type, have, weight)
233	        c.execute(
234	            "UPDATE inventory SET weight = weight - ?, updated_at = ? WHERE report_id = ? AND tovar_turi = ?",
235	            (weight, now, report_id, from_type),
236	        )
237	        c.execute(
238	            "UPDATE inventory SET weight = weight + ?, updated_at = ? WHERE report_id = ? AND tovar_turi = ?",
239	            (weight, now, report_id, to_type),
240	        )

242	            """INSERT INTO activity(report_id, ts, actor, action, from_type, to_type, weight, photos)
243	               VALUES(?, ?, ?, 'adjust', ?, ?, ?, ?)""",
244	            (report_id, now, actor, from_type, to_type, weight, photos),
245	        )
246	        return {"balances": _inventory(c, report_id), "entry_id": cur.lastrowid}

251	# --------------------------------------------------------------------------
252	_EPS = 1e-9  # float compensation slack: don't 409 on rounding dust
253

255	def _get_entry(c: sqlite3.Connection, report_id: int, entry_id: int, action: str):
256	    row = c.execute(
257	        "SELECT * FROM activity WHERE id = ? AND report_id = ? AND action = ?",
258	        (entry_id, report_id, action),
259	    ).fetchone()
260	    if row is None:
261	        raise ActivityNotFound(entry_id)
262	    return row

265	def _apply_balances(c: sqlite3.Connection, report_id: int, deltas: dict[str, float]) -> dict[str, float]:
266	    """Apply per-type deltas; raise InsufficientStock if any balance would go
267	    negative. All-or-nothing (checked before any write)."""
268	    now = int(time.time())
269	    for t in deltas:
270	        _ensure_type(c, report_id, t)
271	    inv = _inventory(c, report_id)
272	    for t, d in deltas.items():
273	        if inv.get(t, 0) + d < -_EPS:
274	            raise InsufficientStock(t, inv.get(t, 0), -d)
275	    for t, d in deltas.items():
276	        if d:
277	            c.execute(
278	                "UPDATE inventory SET weight = weight + ?, updated_at = ? WHERE report_id = ? AND tovar_turi = ?",
279	                (d, now, report_id, t),
280	            )
281	    return _inventory(c, report_id)
282	
283	
284	def edit_reys(report_id: int, entry_id: int, tovar_turi: str, weight: float,
285	              coefficient: float, net: float, photos: int) -> dict:
286	    if not (math.isfinite(weight) and math.isfinite(coefficient) and math.isfinite(net)):
287	        raise ValueError("non-finite value")
288	    with _db() as c:
289	        old = _get_entry(c, report_id, entry_id, "reys")
290	        deltas: dict[str, float] = {}
291	        deltas[old["tovar_turi"]] = deltas.get(old["tovar_turi"], 0) - (old["net"] or 0)
292	        deltas[tovar_turi] = deltas.get(tovar_turi, 0) + net
293	        balances = _apply_balances(c, report_id, deltas)
294	        c.execute(
295	            "UPDATE activity SET tovar_turi = ?, weight = ?, coefficient = ?, net = ?, photos = ? WHERE id = ?",
296	            (tovar_turi, weight, coefficient, net, photos, entry_id),
297	        )
298	        return {"balance": balances.get(tovar_turi, 0), "inventory": balances}
299	
300	
301	def edit_adjust(report_id: int, entry_id: int, from_type: str, to_type: str,
302	                weight: float, photos: int) -> dict:
303	    if not (math.isfinite(weight) and weight > 0):
304	        raise ValueError("non-finite value")
305	    with _db() as c:
306	        old = _get_entry(c, report_id, entry_id, "adjust")
307	        deltas: dict[str, float] = {}
308	        # Reverse the old transfer, then apply the new one.
309	        for t, d in ((old["from_type"], old["weight"] or 0), (old["to_type"], -(old["weight"] or 0)),
310	                     (from_type, -weight), (to_type, weight)):
311	            deltas[t] = deltas.get(t, 0) + d
312	        balances = _apply_balances(c, report_id, deltas)
313	        c.execute(
314	            "UPDATE activity SET from_type = ?, to_type = ?, weight = ?, photos = ? WHERE id = ?",
315	            (from_type, to_type, weight, photos, entry_id),
316	        )
317	        return {"balances": balances}
318	
319	
320	def delete_entry(report_id: int, entry_id: int) -> dict:
321	    """Delete a reys/adjust entry and undo its inventory effect."""
322	    with _db() as c:
323	        row = c.execute(
324	            "SELECT * FROM activity WHERE id = ? AND report_id = ?", (entry_id, report_id)
325	        ).fetchone()
326	        if row is None:
327	            raise ActivityNotFound(entry_id)
328	        if row["action"] == "reys":
329	            deltas = {row["tovar_turi"]: -(row["net"] or 0)}
330	        else:  # adjust: give the weight back to from_type, take it from to_type
331	            deltas = {row["from_type"]: row["weight"] or 0, row["to_type"]: -(row["weight"] or 0)}
332	        balances = _apply_balances(c, report_id, deltas)
333	        c.execute("DELETE FROM activity WHERE id = ?", (entry_id,))
334	        return {"balances": balances}

344	        params.append(actor)
345	    if ts_from is not None:
346	        q += " AND ts >= ?"
347	        params.append(int(ts_from))
348	    if ts_to is not None:
349	        q += " AND ts < ?"

351	    q += " ORDER BY id DESC LIMIT ?"
352	    params.append(limit)
353	    with _db() as c:

findings.summary: The oversized-body rejection middleware `_limit_body` only checks `request.method == "POST"`, so the new multipart endpoints PUT /api/report/{entry_id} and PUT /api/adjust/{entry_id} completely bypass the MAX_BODY_BYTES (62 MB) cap. FastAPI resolves the Form/UploadFile parameters by parsing the entire multipart body (spooling file parts to temp files) BEFORE the handler runs, i.e. before `_rate_ok` and `_auth_or_403` execute, so the cap the middleware comment promises ("reject oversized request bodies BEFORE FastAPI buffers/spools them") does not apply to PUT at all; the handler-level `_read_capped` 413s only fire after the whole body has already been ingested.
findings.scenario: An unauthenticated client sends `PUT /api/report/1` (or /api/adjust/1) with a multi-gigabyte multipart body (e.g. curl -X PUT -F 'photos=@huge.bin'). The server streams and spools the entire body to disk temp files before any auth/rate-limit code runs, then returns 413/403 — repeated or slow-streamed requests exhaust disk/bandwidth. The identical body sent as POST is rejected up front with 413 by the middleware; the PUT variant added in this diff is unprotected. Fix: extend the method check to POST/PUT (any body-bearing method).

findings.summary: In-flight edit collision: the success paths of onSave/onAdjustSave unconditionally clear the editing state (`editingReys = null` at ~line 1747, `editingAdj = null` at ~line 1560) and call resetForm()/resetAdjust(), but nothing prevents the user from starting a second edit while a PUT is still in flight — only the save button is disabled; the "Yuklanganlar" viewer, kebab menu and startEditReys/startEditAdjust remain fully usable (they have no `saving`/`adjusting` guard).
findings.scenario: Edit reys entry A that carries several large photos (every save re-uploads all photos, so the PUT takes seconds on mobile). While "Saqlanmoqda…" is showing, open Yuklanganlar → kebab → Tahrirlash on entry B: startEditReys(B) loads B into the form and sets editingReys = B. When A's PUT then resolves, the success branch nulls editingReys and resetForm() wipes B's just-loaded values/photos (revoking their object URLs); setBusy(false) relabels the button "Saqlash". The user re-enters their changes and taps save → the request goes out as POST /api/report, creating a duplicate new entry on the backend instead of PUT-updating entry B (B's intended edit is silently lost). Same flow applies to adjust edits.

findings.summary: Password minimum-length check was relaxed from 8 to 4 characters but the error message still says "password must be at least 8 characters", so the CLI now prints a wrong requirement when rejecting a too-short password.
findings.scenario: Run `python -m app.passwords someuser` and enter a 3-character PIN: the tool rejects it with "password must be at least 8 characters" even though a 4-character PIN (the intended new minimum) is accepted — the admin is misled into choosing an 8+ char password or believing 4-digit PINs are unsupported.
